transcript: Hi! Yeah, my name’s Wayne Frisbee. I got your number from the gal over at Shelmerdine’s Shrubbery Center up here!—

So, yeah, I live nearby and I just needed a little gardenin’ help up here if you had any availability for some consultation or just some advice—for a neighborly advice session kinda thing, so [chuckles] [pitch-up]—
—Yeah, so I got a w—oh, yeah! [chuckles] Okay—
—[pitch-up] Sure, yeah! So I got a little— [ringing] [chuckles] I’m Wayne Frisbee—and you’re, ah, Jillian?
Yeah! Yeah, I talked to Flo up ‘ere! [exhales]
Okay! Uh, what can I, ah, help you with?
I’m tryin’ to get some cactuses up ’n runnin’ here; I got about 36 of ‘em on my property here.

But these are coated with some special—[pitch-down]—I don’t know what they’re called with but they’re coated to withstand just about any temperature. Any temperature at all! So, [ringing] they said that you were nearby and you’re an expert on this thing. Got a real green thumb [ringing] on this kinda thing, here! [ringing]''
I’m sorry! You said they’re “coated” with some kinda “temperature”?
—A repellant of some kind. Oh, sure, yeah! They got a repellant on there of some kind. I don’t know what it is—
—[pitch-down] A repellant of some kind. Oh—huh? [ringing]—
[ringing] I’m just tryin’ to get ‘em up ’n runnin’! [ringing]
—like minus, ah, ten outside right now—
—It’s not really, ah, the growing season; that’s probably why [chuckles]—
—[pitch-down] [ringing] What do I need to do over here? [pitch-up] [ringing]
—It’s winter; there’s about a foot of snow right now, so they’re not gonna grow right now for you.
Oh no? What if I just wait it out? The whole “waiting game”? Wait a real long time?—
—Yeah! Yeah, you’ll be waiting until the snow melts. [chuckles] Yeah, we have, like, a foot of snow. They’re not gonna grow!
—Hold on a second! Okay. Hello? So, you—are you going to come to pick it up?
No, I’m—I’m—no, I’m not gonna look at them because they’re not gonna grow. It’s winter!—

—Oh, between eleven and one o’clock. You got it! Why? Eh, that’s fine with me, I’m not—listen! I’m not gonna force you to take what you don’t want! Of course not! You don’t want it, you don’t want it! That’s fine with me!
Eh, just bring a few boxes, you know, I—I’m gonna pay you for those boxes! What are you worried about it?!
—You should always have boxes that you offer it up the way you want it and that’s the end of it!
Um, I’m—I was talkin’, ah, Mister Frisbee, there—
—That is correct! You need the big boxes! Eh?
—I’m—I’m yelling. This’s the way I talk. When you’ll be 95 you’re gonna talk like me too!
Okay, I’m sorry. I’m sorry if that—that offended you. Okay. Okay, so you’re gonna keep it—eleven of ‘em. You got it!—
—So, I was talkin’ ta Wayne Frisbee, there, ah, just a moment ago.
You don’t need five or six boxes.
I don’t understand what you’re saying!
Yeah, okay! Okay! No problem! I know what I’m talkin’ about?
What the fuck’s goin’ on? Hello? What the hell is this?—
Uh, I think you have the wrong number.
I don’t know why you’re calling me, then.
I didn’t call you. What the heck?
Hello? What is that sound? Sorry—
—Who are you, anyway?! Who are you, anyway?!
What? Who are you? What the h—
What do you think you’re talking to? Hello?—
—If I don’t know you, why would I wanna give you my name?
You called me! You don’t give me your name!—
You didn’t give me your name!
But I wanna know who you are before I give you my name!
I never called you at all. What is your phone number?!
I’m not giving you my phone number!
You don’t wanna give me your phone number?
—Okay, my name i—my name is Joseph Sauer.
Now do you know who I am?—
—So, why are you calling me? Why are you calling me and getting mad at me for your mistake? Who do you think I am?—
—Who do—who do you—who do you think—who do you—who do you think you’re talking to?
I’m not the bigshot! I’m 95 years of age!—
—Who do you think?—Okay, well, you got the wrong number.
Jeez! What the fuck was that?—
—Are we all squared away or…? So are we all squared away or…?
